title: Privacy, Security & Trust

acceptanceScenarios.name: Create durable trust packet
acceptanceScenarios.given: The tenant has assistant governance packets, agent governance packets, observability incidents, audit events, automation policies, action queue items, and external SCRI/security/privacy events.
acceptanceScenarios.when: A privacy, security, or trust owner opens Sprint 3 and clicks Create Sprint 3 packet.
acceptanceScenarios.then: The app persists an AssistantPrivacySecurityTrustPacket with consent posture, DSR, data transfer, identity access, security exception, threat exposure, trust assurance, response plan, rollback plan, and leadership summary.

acceptanceScenarios.name: Queue trust review
acceptanceScenarios.given: A Sprint 3 packet has consent gaps, DSR blockers, transfer risk, identity risk, security exceptions, or threat exposure.
acceptanceScenarios.when: The user adds a note and clicks Queue review.
acceptanceScenarios.then: The packet moves to REVIEW_QUEUED, an AssistantActionQueueItem is created, and an AssistantAuditEvent records that trust remediation is approval-gated.

acceptanceScenarios.name: Approve without silent mutation
acceptanceScenarios.given: A human reviewer has validated privacy, security, identity, and trust evidence.
acceptanceScenarios.then: The packet is marked APPROVED with approver metadata while consent, DSR, transfer posture, identity, entitlements, security exceptions, threat responses, automation policies, and operational source records remain unchanged.

guardrails: Packet creation never changes consent records, user/customer preferences, DSR exports, deletion/anonymization state, data-transfer posture, role grants, entitlements, tool scopes, security exceptions, threat responses, or operational records.
Security and privacy outputs are triage and assurance evidence only until a human queues and approves downstream work.
Action queue handoff and audit events are explicit for every review or approval.
